bioHotLightweight Curation — AI selection & significance rating
← bioHot

Systems Biology & Network Analysis

Gene regulatory network inference, metabolic network modeling (FBA), and multi-omics integration frameworks.

20 papers curated · High: 4 · Medium: 16 · Low: 0 · Updated: 2026-07-10

bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

A deep-learning-informed prior and Bayesian model for differential AP-MS interactome analysis

This paper introduces BayesInteractomics, a new computational framework that combines deep learning with Bayesian statistics to improve the analysis of affinity-purification mass spectrometry (AP-MS) data. By using a neural network trained on protein structures to predict direct binding and incorporating this as prior knowledge, the method better distinguishes genuine protein-protein interactions from background noise compared to existing tools.

proteomicsmachine-learningtool
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

scJET: Full-gene Space Single-cell Expression Generation with Patch-based Transformer Modeling

scJET is a new deep learning method that generates realistic full-gene expression data for single cells, overcoming the limitations of previous models that only use a subset of highly variable genes. It uses a patch-based Transformer approach to denoise and generate data while preserving both global cell relationships and local gene-level patterns. This provides a more complete and efficient framework for generating transcriptome-wide single-cell data.

single-celltranscriptomicsdeep-learning
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

SPECTER-Based Semantic Triage of Biomedical Literature for Systematic Reviews in Mutational Signature Analysis

This paper introduces a reproducible pipeline that uses SPECTER embeddings and similarity metrics to automatically rank biomedical literature for systematic reviews, specifically for mutational signature analysis. Tested on over 2,200 records, it outperforms keyword-only and TF-IDF methods, reducing manual screening effort while maintaining high recall.

machine-learningtoolmethodology
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

BBBP_Atlas: Unified Interpretable Modeling of Blood Brain Barrier Permeability across Small Molecules and Peptides

The BBBP_Atlas is a new computational resource that unifies fragmented blood-brain barrier permeability data and provides interpretable machine learning models applicable to both small molecules and peptides, overcoming limitations of previous descriptor-based and small-molecule-only approaches. It addresses data leakage and standardization issues in existing datasets, enabling more reliable predictions for central nervous system drug discovery.

drug-discoverymachine-learningdatabase
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

Mutational bias shapes protein evolution across RNA viruses

This study shows that mutational biases, not just natural selection, play a major role in shaping protein evolution in RNA viruses. Using SARS-CoV-2 as a model, the authors demonstrate that the virus's mutational spectrum predicts over 50% of observed amino acid substitutions and overall changes in protein composition during the pandemic.

evolutiongenomicsmethodology
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

Metabolic Rewiring in Triple-Negative Breast Cancer: Systems Analysis of TCGA-BRCA Transcriptome Reveals Prognostic Hub Genes

This study uses a systems biology approach to analyze metabolic rewiring in triple-negative breast cancer (TNBC) by examining transcriptomic data from TCGA-BRCA. The authors identify 764 differentially expressed genes and key metabolic hub genes, with ATP1A2 emerging as a significant prognostic marker. The work maps coordinated metabolic pathways that may underlie TNBC's aggressive behavior, offering potential targets for prognosis and therapy.

transcriptomicssystems-biologycancer
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) High

Learning the spatial cell-cell communication network to decode multi-channel signaling and predict network-hub vulnerabilities with MOSANIC

MOSANIC is a new computational method that learns spatial cell-cell communication networks from multi-channel imaging data, enabling the identification of signaling hubs and prediction of network vulnerabilities. By modeling the network as a whole rather than individual interactions, it can reveal how signals propagate through intermediate cells and predict tissue-wide responses to node loss. This approach provides a systems-level view of intercellular signaling that could identify rational intervention points in disease.

spatial-omicsnetwork-analysistool
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

Assessing tensor decomposition quality of immune profiling data from a dictionary learning perspective

This paper introduces new metrics for assessing the quality of tensor decompositions applied to immune profiling data, specifically for Non-negative CANDECOMP/PARAFAC (NCPD) decompositions. By considering the experimental data model, the authors demonstrate how these metrics can guide the selection of rank and trial, and they reframe results through a dictionary learning perspective to improve interpretability.

methodologymachine-learningimmunology
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

Generative AI Models Reveal Dynamic Views of Aging (DyViA) Phenotypes in Healthy Individuals

This paper introduces DyViA, a generative AI platform that uses diffusion and GAN models to create personalized aging trajectories for healthy individuals. The platform can predict how specific phenotypes (e.g., physical or cognitive measures) might change over time, offering a new tool for studying healthy aging.

machine-learningdeep-learningaging
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

A five-dimensional functional state space for fingerprinting disease transcriptomes

This paper introduces a five-dimensional functional state space that maps disease transcriptomes onto coordinated activity patterns across major biological systems, enabling systematic comparison across conditions. The framework provides a reproducible pipeline for functional scoring and cross-condition analysis, addressing the challenge of fragmented gene and pathway lists in transcriptomics.

transcriptomicsmethodologysystems-biology
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

Gene-specific exponent-corrected normalization for library size in bulk RNA-seq

This paper identifies that standard library size normalization methods for bulk RNA-seq often fail to fully remove correlation between gene expression counts and sequencing depth in large datasets. The authors propose a gene-specific exponent-corrected normalization approach that better accounts for library size effects, improving differential expression analysis across over 100 public datasets.

transcriptomicsmethodologytool
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) High

IgGM2: An All-Atom Foundation Model for Adaptive Immune Receptor Design

IgGM2 is a new AI model that can simultaneously predict the 3D structure and design the amino-acid sequence of immune receptors like antibodies and T-cell receptors, handling all atoms. Unlike previous methods that treat structure and sequence separately or focus on one receptor type, IgGM2 unifies these tasks for more accurate immune receptor design. This could accelerate the development of new therapeutic antibodies and T-cell-based therapies.

deep-learningprotein-structuredrug-discovery
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

Gene Program Negotiation Defines Cellular Identity in Single-Cell Transcriptomes

This paper introduces Gene Program Negotiation (GPN), a graph-based computational framework that models how multiple simultaneously active gene programs collectively determine cellular identity in single-cell transcriptomes, overcoming the limitations of existing methods that analyze programs independently. By reconstructing cell-specific program interactions, GPN provides deeper insight into the regulatory decision-making underlying cellular heterogeneity.

single-celltranscriptomicsmethodology
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

Rectangle: robust and scalable multiscale deconvolution informed by single-cell RNA sequencing data

Rectangle is a new Python framework for deconvolving bulk RNA-seq data using single-cell references. It improves resolution of closely related cell types, scales efficiently to large datasets, and accounts for unknown cellular content not present in the reference.

transcriptomicssingle-celltool
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

Mind the Alignment Gap: A Spatial Transcriptomics Benchmark for Scientific Coding Agents

This paper introduces a framework for automatically building benchmarks to evaluate scientific coding agents, using spatial transcriptomics alignment as a case study. The authors construct 40 tasks from peer-reviewed papers where agents must produce coordinate tables, addressing the challenge of verifying ambiguous outputs in scientific research tasks.

spatial-omicstoolmachine-learning
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) High

Coding agents author interpretable single-cell embedding models from the literature

This paper presents a method where coding agents automatically generate interpretable single-cell embedding models by extracting marker-gene programs directly from the literature, rather than learning cell-state structure from scratch. This approach produces sparse, named dimensions that require no post-hoc interpretation or batch correction, improving interpretability and domain specificity.

single-cellmachine-learningtool
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

LeafRank: A phylodynamic framework for inferring relative fitness from single-cell phylogenies in chromosomally unstable tumors

LeafRank is a new computational method that uses single-cell DNA sequencing data to infer the relative fitness of individual cancer cells in tumors with chromosomal instability. By modeling tumor evolution as a branching process, it can quantify how different cells contribute to tumor growth, even in complex scenarios like whole-genome duplication.

cancersingle-celltool
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) High

EZSolver: Template-free prediction of polar enzymatic mechanisms via bidirectional flow matching and search

EZSolver is a new generative AI framework that predicts polar enzymatic reaction mechanisms without relying on predefined rule-based templates. It uses a flow matching model (EZFlow) and a bidirectional beam search to learn chemistry directly, enabling it to generalize to novel reactions that traditional methods cannot handle.

machine-learningmethodologystructural-biology
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

BertST: BERT-based Spatial Domain Identification in Patient Data

This paper introduces BertST, a BERT-based method for identifying spatial domains in spatial transcriptomics data. By leveraging a transformer architecture, BertST models both local and long-range spatial dependencies to partition tissue into coherent regions, outperforming existing graph neural network approaches.

spatial-omicstranscriptomicsdeep-learning
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

Thematic Shifts in Early-High-Impact Cancer Genomics and Diagnostics Research: A Bibliometric and Semantic Analysis

This study uses a bibliometric framework and large language models to identify and characterize the most influential recent publications in cancer genomics and diagnostics, revealing thematic shifts in topics that attract early citation prominence. The findings can inform research strategy and clinical translation by highlighting emerging high-impact areas.

cancergenomicsmachine-learning
